mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 4.5 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in housing starts indicate demand shifting toward durable goods and away from construction, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a negative return differential compared to baseline periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, elevated industrial production levels signal capacity constraints and overheating risks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 60 percent of high-INDPRO observations within late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism reverses direction during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 3.0 percent force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of high-inflation trigger events, or the signal shows no decay in predictive accuracy across varying inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands corporate revenue baselines and improves profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the correlation between GDPC1 changes and equity returns falls below 0.10 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In disinflation regimes, rising industrial production alongside stable inflation indicates supply-side recovery without price pressures, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in over 60 percent of disinflation periods, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5 percent.
